common.tagline: Monitor smarter. Alert faster. Stay discoverable.

landing.heroTitle: Website monitoring that developers actually enjoy
landing.heroSubtitle: Uptime checks, SSL and DNS security, content integrity alerts, and CN IM notifications — commercial-friendly free tier with alerts in under 60 seconds.
landing.trustedBy: Trusted by teams building reliable products
landing.foundingSubtitle: Only {count} spots left at founding price
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need to stay online
landing.feature1Desc: HTTP, TCP, Ping, Keyword, SSL expiry, and DNS record monitoring from external probes.
landing.feature2Desc: SSL expiry tiers (30/14/7/1 days), DNS hijack and record-change alerts, plus tamper detection with optional content policy keywords.
landing.feature3Desc: p95 latency trends, spike detection, and proactive SSL warnings before certificates lapse.
landing.feature4Desc: Email, Webhook, Slack, Discord, Teams, DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, SMS, and voice — with per-monitor webhook toggles for flexible routing.
landing.feature5Desc: Incident acknowledgement workflows and optional voice calls so production pages reach someone who can act.
landing.feature6Desc: Run checks from multiple regions to catch geo-specific outages and routing issues early.
landing.aboutLead: PulseWatch is an uptime and website monitoring platform that checks your URLs, APIs, DNS, and certificates on a schedule—and alerts your team when something breaks or drifts.
landing.aboutP1: Beyond basic ping tools, PulseWatch runs HTTP checks with status codes, response-time thresholds, keyword validation, TCP/port probes, ICMP ping, SSL certificate expiry tracking with tiered warnings (30/14/7/1 days), DNS record baselines that detect hijack or drift, and content-integrity monitors with fingerprint diffs and optional policy keyword scans. Each monitor stores check history so you can see p95 latency trends, recent failures, and recovery time without exporting logs to a spreadsheet.
landing.aboutP2: Alerts fire on state changes and security events through email, webhooks, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, DingTalk, Feishu, and WeCom—with per-monitor webhook toggles when you need quieter routing. On-call acknowledgement and voice escalation help teams that cannot miss production incidents. Public status pages let customers see transparency during outages while your engineers work the incident timeline inside PulseWatch.
landing.aboutP3: Built for developers and small SaaS teams: a commercial-friendly free tier (10 monitors, 5-minute minimum interval), multi-region external probes, a dark-mode dashboard, REST API access, free developer tools at /tools, optional WeChat miniprogram access for on-the-go status, and founding-member pricing that stays predictable as you grow. Sign in, add a monitor, and get your first external check within minutes—no agent install required for standard HTTP monitoring.
landing.useCasesSubtitle: From side projects to revenue-critical APIs—if downtime costs you money or trust, external checks are the fastest safety net.
landing.useCase1Desc: Monitor REST and GraphQL health endpoints, auth gateways, and webhooks. Catch 5xx spikes and slow p95 before customers open support tickets.
landing.useCase2Desc: Watch storefront URLs, payment callbacks, and inventory APIs. A two-minute outage during a campaign can dwarf the cost of monitoring.
landing.useCase3Desc: Track admin panels, background workers, and staging environments. Share a branded status page so users know you are already on it.
landing.useCase4Desc: Bilingual UI (English / 中文), native DingTalk, Feishu, and WeCom alert channels, plus webhook flexibility for teams shipping worldwide from 国内.
landing.featuresSubtitle: Uptime checks, SSL and DNS security, content integrity, multi-channel alerts, on-call workflows, and developer tools.
landing.trustLead: Industry studies often cite five-figure USD losses per hour for mid-size online businesses. PulseWatch helps you detect issues in seconds, not after social media does.
landing.trustPoint1: A single undetected API outage during peak traffic can cost more than a year of Pro monitoring.
landing.trustPoint2: SSL expiry surprises still take sites offline—tiered certificate alerts at 30, 14, 7, and 1 days remove calendar roulette.
landing.trustPoint3: DNS drift and page tampering often precede bigger breaches—baseline checks catch changes before customers do.
landing.trustCompare: See how we compare on features and pricing:
landing.faqSubtitle: Answers about uptime monitoring, alerts, pricing, and getting started with PulseWatch.
landing.faq1A: Uptime monitoring runs automated checks from outside your network on a fixed interval (e.g. every 60 seconds). If a check fails—timeout, wrong status code, or missing keyword—you get alerted so you can fix the issue before users churn.
landing.faq2Q: How is PulseWatch different from free ping tools?
landing.faq2A: PulseWatch combines multi-protocol checks, historical metrics, incident workflows, optional status pages, and team-friendly alerting. The free tier includes 10 monitors (5-minute minimum interval) with commercial use allowed—unlike many hobby-only free plans.
landing.faq3Q: Do I need to install an agent on my server?
landing.faq3A: No agent is required for standard HTTP/HTTPS, TCP, ping, keyword, or SSL checks. PulseWatch probes your public endpoints from our infrastructure—ideal for SaaS, APIs, and static sites.
landing.faq4A: Email, webhooks, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, DingTalk, Feishu, and WeCom are supported. Configure channels in Settings → Integrations, then route alerts globally or disable webhooks per monitor when you need finer control. Voice calls are available on Business plans for critical DOWN events.
landing.faq5Q: Can I publish a public status page?
landing.faq5A: Yes. After logging in, create a status page in the dashboard and map monitors to components. Share the public URL with customers while you update incidents internally.
landing.faq6Q: Is the free plan really free for commercial projects?
landing.faq6A: Yes—PulseWatch allows commercial use on the free tier (10 monitors) so early-stage SaaS and client work can start without a credit card. Upgrade when you need more monitors, faster intervals, or team features.
landing.faq7A: Alerts trigger after consecutive failed checks according to your monitor settings—typically within one to two intervals. Recovery notifications fire when service is healthy again.
landing.faq8Q: How do I start monitoring in under five minutes?
landing.faq8A: Register free, click Add monitor, paste your URL, choose HTTP check settings, and save. PulseWatch begins scheduling checks immediately. Log in anytime at /login to view dashboards and incidents.
landing.resourcesAppNote: Monitors, incidents, and status pages are available after sign-in from your authenticated dashboard.
landing.jsonLdOrgDescription: Uptime and website monitoring with SSL, DNS, and content integrity alerts, CN IM channels, and fast incident workflows.
landing.jsonLdPageDescription: Commercial-friendly uptime monitoring with SSL expiry tiers, DNS change detection, tamper alerts, maintenance windows, on-call, DingTalk/Feishu/WeCom, and bilingual support.
landing.toolsCtaDesc: Format JSON, escape strings, encode Base64 and URLs, convert timestamps — all in your browser, no signup.
landing.feature7Desc: REST API with org-scoped keys, clear permissions, and free browser tools at /tools.
landing.feature8Desc: Branded public status pages, scheduled maintenance windows that suppress alerts, incident timelines, and recovery notifications.
landing.feature9Desc: 10 monitors on the free tier with 5-minute checks — hobby and early SaaS friendly, with a premium dark-mode dashboard.
landing.useCase5Desc: Track SSL expiry before browsers warn users, catch DNS record changes that could indicate hijacking, and detect unexpected page tampering or policy-violating content.
landing.faq9Q: What security monitors does PulseWatch offer?
landing.faq9A: Dedicated SSL monitors with tiered expiry warnings (30/14/7/1 days), DNS monitors that baseline records and alert on hijack or drift, and content-integrity (tamper) monitors with fingerprint diffs plus optional gambling or adult-content keyword policies. Security events appear in incidents alongside uptime DOWN alerts.
landing.faq10Q: Can I turn off webhooks for a single monitor?
landing.faq10A: Yes. When creating or editing a monitor, use the webhook toggle in alert settings. Email and other configured channels still fire according to your alert rules; only webhook delivery is skipped for that monitor.
landing.faq11Q: Do you support on-call acknowledgement and voice alerts?
landing.faq11A: Yes. On-call schedules support acknowledgement workflows so the right person confirms they are handling an incident. Voice (TTS phone) alerts are available on Business plans for critical monitor DOWN events, with rate limits to prevent alert storms.
landing.faq12Q: Can I schedule maintenance windows to pause alerts?
landing.faq12A: Yes. In Settings → Maintenance windows, define planned downtime for your organization. Checks may still run, but alert noise is suppressed so on-call is not paged for expected work.
landing.faq13Q: Is there a command palette or competitor comparison pages?
landing.faq13A: Signed-in users can press ⌘K (Ctrl+K on Windows) to jump to monitors, incidents, settings, and more. Public compare pages at /compare/uptimerobot and /compare/better-stack summarize how PulseWatch stacks up on pricing and features.
landing.capabilitiesTitle: Platform capabilities for uptime, security, and on-call
landing.capabilitiesSubtitle: External probes, tiered SSL alerts, DNS and tamper detection, multi-channel notifications, status pages, and developer-friendly workflows — all indexable, all built for production teams.
landing.cap1Desc: HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, Ping, and keyword checks from external multi-region probes. Catch geo-specific routing failures and latency spikes before your users do.
landing.cap2Title: SSL certificate expiry with tiered warnings
landing.cap2Desc: Dedicated SSL monitors with graduated alerts at 30, 14, 7, and 1 days before expiry — so renewals happen on your schedule, not when browsers turn red.
landing.cap3Desc: Baseline DNS records and alert on drift, unexpected record changes, or signs of hijacking — a first line of defense before traffic is redirected.
landing.cap4Desc: Fingerprint page content, detect major unauthorized changes, and optionally scan for policy-violating keywords (e.g. gambling or adult content insertion).
landing.cap5Desc: Incident timelines with acknowledgement workflows, on-call schedules, optional SMS and voice (TTS) escalation on Business plans — reach someone who can act.
landing.cap6Title: Alert channels: email to enterprise IM
landing.cap6Desc: Email, webhooks, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams, DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, SMS, and voice calls. Route globally or mute webhooks per monitor.
landing.cap7Desc: Publish branded public status pages for customers. Schedule maintenance windows to suppress alert noise during planned deploys and infrastructure work.
landing.cap8Desc: Browser-based developer tools at /tools including a free website down checker and SSL checker (no signup). Power users jump anywhere with ⌘K / Ctrl+K. Compare PulseWatch vs UptimeRobot or Better Stack on public pages.
landing.cap9Desc: Signed-in users can check monitor status on the go via the WeChat miniprogram (微信小程序查看) — uptime visibility without opening a laptop.
landing.resourcesMiniprogramNote: WeChat miniprogram (微信小程序) available for signed-in users to check monitor status on mobile.

monitors.title: Monitors
monitors.subtitle: Manage your websites and endpoints
monitors.emptyDesc: Add your first monitor to start tracking uptime.
monitors.heartbeatCreatedDesc: Send POST requests to this URL on your schedule (e.g. cron):
monitors.aiDraftDesc: Describe what you want to watch. AI fills monitor type, URL, interval, and advanced config.
monitors.aiDraftPlaceholder: e.g. Check my login API every minute. POST JSON to /login and treat 200 or 401 as healthy.
monitors.aiDraftEditDesc: Describe what to change in plain language. AI updates name, URL, interval, and advanced config. Monitor type stays as-is.
monitors.aiDraftEditPlaceholder: e.g. Check every 60 seconds, POST JSON to /api/health, and accept 200 or 503.
monitors.typeReadonly: Monitor type cannot be changed after creation
monitors.pagespeedConfigDesc: Set alert thresholds for user-experience and navigation timing metrics.
monitors.sslWarnDaysHint: Alerts at 30, 14, 7, and 1 day thresholds within this window.
monitors.dnsBaselineAuto: Auto — capture on first successful check
monitors.dnsBaselineManual: Manual — use expected value or capture baseline
monitors.dnsExpectedHint: Static assertion: record set must contain this value.
monitors.dnsTrustedResolversHint: Comma-separated IPs. When two or more are set, all must agree or the check fails.
monitors.tamperAIRecognitionDesc: Send visible page text to DeepSeek to identify defacement, phishing, gambling, adult content, and suspicious injected copy.
monitors.tamperAIIntervalHint: Free plan runs AI recognition every 30 minutes. Paid plans can run as fast as every 30 seconds.
monitors.tamperAIFreeInterval: AI recognition is limited to every 30 minutes on the free plan.
monitors.freePlanIntervalHint: Free plan: minimum 5-minute check interval. Upgrade for 1-minute or 30-second checks.
monitors.tamperAIPaidInterval: Paid plans can run AI recognition as fast as every 30 seconds.
monitors.tamperContentConsent: I consent to automated content scanning (see Privacy Policy)
monitors.tamperFalsePositiveHint: Rule-based matching may produce false positives on news, legal, or educational sites.
monitors.expectedStatusHint: Comma-separated HTTP status codes. Check passes if response matches any of them.
monitors.enableRequestChainHelp:
What it solves
Protected APIs often return 401/403 on a direct probe, causing false DOWN alerts.

Use when
• Login first, then health-check (session / JWT)
• Exchange a token before calling the API
• Multi-hop flows (e.g. create, then poll status)

How it works
Up to 5 ordered steps. Extract values from each response and reference them as {{varName}} in later URLs, headers, or body.
monitors.requestChainHint: Run requests in order. Use {{varName}} in URL, headers, or body. Extract values from each step for the next.
monitors.chainDemoDesc: Step 1 logs in and extracts a token; step 2 calls a protected API with {{token}}. Click to auto-fill.
monitors.chainBodyHint: JSON body; use {{varName}} for values from previous steps
monitors.chainExtractHint: e.g. var token, source JSON, path accessToken
monitors.targetUrlChainHint: For single requests, this is the check URL. Step 1 of a chain uses this URL when left empty.
monitors.stepUrlOptional: URL (optional — uses target URL for step 1)
monitors.timingBreakdownDesc: DNS, TCP connect, TLS, server response (TTFB), and download time for the latest check.
monitors.pagespeedInsightsDesc: Navigation timing, estimated Web Vitals, page weight, and resource inventory for the latest check.
monitors.pagespeedNoResources: No linked resources discovered in HTML
monitors.pagespeedHtmlSnapshotDesc: Current MVP stores HTML-level size and resource inventory; full browser element timing is planned.
monitors.checkSearchPlaceholder: Search error message or status code...
monitors.consecutiveFailuresHint: Alert and incident only after this many failed checks in a row (1–10). Until then the monitor shows as pending.
monitors.webhookAlertsEnabled: Send Webhook alerts for this monitor
monitors.webhookAlertsHint: When off, generic Webhook integrations in Settings will not fire for this monitor. Email, Slack, and other channels are unaffected.
monitors.apiJsonHint: At least one JSONPath assertion is required. Available on Pro and above.
monitors.forensicsDesc: Screenshots and HTTP capture from recent failed checks.
monitors.aiVisualDesc: Explain page risk using recent screenshot evidence, errors, and tamper metadata.

settings.ssoDesc: Configure OpenID Connect for Business plan members. Login slug: {slug}

settings.stripeNotConfigured: Stripe is not configured. Please contact an administrator.

integrations.desc: Configure Webhook, Slack, Discord, Teams, DingTalk, Feishu, or WeCom to receive alerts when monitors go down, recover, or for security events (SSL, DNS change, content integrity).
integrations.voiceHint: Business plan. TTS phone call on DOWN (15 min rate limit).
integrations.webhookHint: Slack/Discord/Teams: use Incoming Webhook URL from your workspace settings.
integrations.dingtalkHint: DingTalk group robot: Custom → Robot → Webhook. Paste the full URL (includes access_token). Enable signing in robot security settings and paste the SEC secret below.
integrations.feishuHint: Feishu/Lark group bot: Settings → Bots → Add bot → Custom bot. Copy the webhook URL. Enable signature verification and paste the secret below.
integrations.wecomHint: WeCom group robot: Group chat → ··· → Group bot → Add. Copy webhook URL (includes key). WeCom robots do not support HMAC signing; configure IP allowlist in WeCom admin if required.
integrations.signSecretPlaceholder: SEC… or bot secret from platform
integrations.signHint: Required when the robot has signature verification enabled on DingTalk or Feishu.
integrations.smsHint: Requires TWILIO_* env vars on the server.
integrations.eventTypesHint: Select one or more event types, or choose All events to receive every alert.
integrations.delayMinutes: Alert delay (minutes — notify after DOWN for N minutes)
integrations.noChannels: No integration channels yet. Email alerts use your account email by default.
